title: Apply: anatomical evidence of evolution
description: Apply your understanding of anatomical evidence of evolution in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
Compare the two animal structures described below. Then, decide if the structures are homologous or analogous.

Sharks and dolphins have structures that help them swim in ocean water. In sharks, these structures are called fins. In dolphins, they are called flippers.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Both dolphins and sharks are animals with backbones. However, dolphins are mammals, and sharks are fish. This means that dolphins, but not sharks, evolved from an ancient land mammal.

**Are shark fins and dolphin flippers most likely homologous or analogous structures?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
"[Tiburón azul (Prionace glauca), canal Fayal-Pico, islas Azores, Portugal, 2020-07-27, DD 28](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tibur%C3%B3n_azul_(Prionace_glauca),_canal_Fayal-Pico,_islas_Azores,_Portugal,_2020-07-27,_DD_28.jpg)" by Diego Delso, [CC BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/)

"[A spinner dolphin in the Red Sea](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:A_spinner_dolphin_in_the_Red_Sea.jpg)" by Alexander Vasenin, [CC BY-SA 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/deed.en)

options.alt: A shark swimming in blue water.
options.caption: *a shark with fins*

options.alt: A dolphin swimming in blue water.
options.caption: *a dolphin with flippers*

assessment_items.question_content:
Compare the two animal structures described below. Then, decide if the structures are homologous or analogous.

Platypuses and ducks have bills that help them catch fish underwater. Both platypus and duck bills can sense the movement of prey.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Both platypuses and ducks are animals with backbones. However, platypuses are mammals, and ducks are birds. This means that the most recent common ancestor of platypuses and ducks did not have a bill.

**Are platypus and duck bills most likely homologous or analogous structures?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
"[Em - Ornithorhynchus anatinus - 3](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Em_-_Ornithorhynchus_anatinus_-_3.jpg)" by Emőke Dénes, [CC BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/deed.en)

"[White-Faced Whistling Duck (1333738076)](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:White-Faced_Whistling_Duck_(1333738076).jpg)" by Jean, [CC BY 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/deed.en)

options.caption: *a platypus with a flat, wide bill*
*a duck with a flat, black bill*

assessment_items.question_content:
Compare the two animal structures described below. Then, decide if the structures are homologous or analogous.

Bats and spider monkeys use their front limbs to move around their environment. The front limbs of bats are wings. The front limbs of spider monkeys are arms. Both bat wings and spider monkey arms have a similar bone structure.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Both bats and spider monkeys are four-limbed mammals. They share a common ancestor that was also a four-limbed mammal.

**Are bat wings and spider monkey arms likely homologous or analogous structures?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
"[Southern bentwing bat](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Southern_bentwing_bat.jpg)" by Steve Bourne, [CC BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/deed.en)

"[Spider Monkey, Tortuguero](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Spider_Monkey,_Tortuguero.jpg)" by Arturo de Frias Marques, [CC BY-SA 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/deed.en)

options.alt: A bat flying with outstretched wings.
options.caption: *a bat*

options.alt: A spider monkey getting food from a tree.
options.caption: *a spider monkey*

assessment_items.question_content:
According to scientists, pine trees share more homologous features with strawberry plants than they do with ferns.

**Which evolutionary tree correctly shows the relationship between pines, strawberries, and ferns?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: Species that share *more* homologous features are *more* closely related.

The text tells us that pines share more homologous features with strawberries than they do with ferns. So, the correct evolutionary tree will show that pines are more closely related to strawberries than they are to ferns.
In other words, the correct evolutionary tree will show that pines and strawberries share a common ancestor in the more recent past compared to pines and ferns.

The correct answer is:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled pine at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled strawberry. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled fern.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/34610fe88fe882f4428f58a1d8c8af7f.png)

![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled fern at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled pine. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled strawberry.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2b6dfc958a590695515296d35a211de2.png)

![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled strawberry at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled fern. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled pine.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/164f02063f0824353f0a761e37a29829.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
According to scientists, cactuses share more homologous features with pumpkin plants than they do with wheat plants.

**Which evolutionary tree correctly shows the relationship between cactuses, pumpkins, and wheat?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: Species that share *more* homologous features are *more* closely related.

The text tells us that cactuses share more homologous features with pumpkins than they do with wheat. So, the correct evolutionary tree will show that cactuses are more closely related to pumpkins than they are to wheat.
In other words, the correct evolutionary tree will show that cactuses and pumpkins share a common ancestor in the more recent past compared to cactuses and wheat.

The correct answer is:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled pumpkin at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled cactus. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled wheat.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6b585bbe5b842b236e37c591fec83a38.png)

![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled wheat at the top. assessment_items.question_content:
The table below shows whether or not a set of organisms has four limbs, a gizzard, and an amniotic egg. A plus sign (+) indicates that an organism has the feature.

  | four limbs | gizzard | amniotic egg
- | :-: | :-: | :-:
frog | + |   |  
alligator | + | + | +
bird | + | + | +

**Based on the table, which two organisms are most closely related?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Species that share *more* anatomical features tend to be *more* closely related.

The table tells us that:  

* Alligators and frogs both have four limbs.
* Birds and frogs both have four limbs.
* Birds and alligators both have four limbs, a gizzard, and an amniotic egg.
This means that birds and alligators share more anatomical features with each other than they do with frogs.
So, based on the table, the two organisms that are most closely related are birds and alligators.

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below shows whether or not a set of organisms has an opposable thumb, mammary glands, and a rotating shoulder. A plus sign (+) indicates that an organism has the feature.

  | opposable thumb | mammary glands | rotating shoulder
- | :-: | :-: | :-:
gorilla | + | +  | + 
cow |   | + |  
bonobo | + | + | +

**Based on the table, which two organisms are most closely related?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Species that share *more* anatomical features tend to be *more* closely related.

The table tells us that: 

* Gorillas and cows both have mammary glands.
* Bonobos and cows both have mammary glands.
* Gorillas and bonobos both have mammary glands, opposable thumbs, and rotating shoulders.
This means that gorillas and bonobos share more anatomical features with each other than they do with cows.
So, based on the table, the two organisms that are most closely related are gorillas and bonobos.

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below shows whether or not a set of organisms has seeds, megaphylls, and vascular tissue. A plus sign (+) indicates that an organism has the feature.

  | seeds | megaphylls | vascular tissue
- | :-: | :-: | :-:
redwood tree | + | +  | + 
primrose | +  | + | + 
fern |   | + | +

**Based on the table, which two organisms are most closely related?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Species that share *more* anatomical features tend to be *more* closely related.

The table tells us that:  

* Primroses and ferns both have megaphylls and vascular tissue.
* Redwood trees and ferns both have megaphylls and vascular tissue.
* Redwood trees and primroses both have megaphylls, vascular tissue, and seeds.
This means that redwood trees and primroses share more anatomical features with each other than they do with ferns.
So, based on the table, the two organisms that are most closely related are redwood trees and primroses.

assessment_items.question_content:
The images below show an elephant and a woolly mammoth. Elephants are alive today. Woolly mammoths are extinct.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Scientists estimate that the elephant and woolly mammoth lineages branched off from a common ancestor roughly 6 million years ago. 

**Which features can provide evidence that elephants and woolly mammoths evolved from a common ancestor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
"[Elephant Addo](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Elephant_Addo.jpg)" by Brian Snelson, [CC BY2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/deed.en)

"[MprimigeniusAalborg1](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:MprimigeniusAalborg1.jpg)" by Honymand, [CC BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/deed.en)

options.alt: An elephant with rough, gray skin, four legs, a trunk, a flat head, and tusks.
options.caption: *an elephant*

options.alt: A model of a woolly mammoth showing that woolly mammoths had shaggy brown fur, four legs, a trunk, a rounded head, and tusks.
options.caption: *a model of a woolly mammoth, recreated from fossil evidence*

assessment_items.question_content:
The drawings below depict a chicken and an *Archaeopteryx*. Chickens are alive today. *Archaeopteryx* are extinct.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Scientists estimate that the chicken and *Archaeopteryx* lineages branched off from a common ancestor roughly 155 million years ago.

**Which features can provide evidence that chickens and *Archaeopteryx* evolved from a common ancestor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
"[202104 Chicken](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:202104_Chicken.svg)" by DataBase Center for Life Science (DBCLS), [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/deed.en)

"[202010 Archaeopteryx lithographica](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:202010_Archaeopteryx_lithographica.png)" by DataBase Center for Life Science (DBCLS), [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/deed.en)

options.alt: A drawing of a chicken with white feathers, two legs, and a broad, fanlike tail.
options.caption: *a drawing of a chicken*

options.alt: A drawing of an Archaeopteryx, which is a bird-like dinosaur. The Archaeopteryx is shown with black feathers, a long, straight tail, two legs, and claws on the ends of its wings.
options.caption: *a drawing of Archaeopteryx, recreated from fossil evidence*

assessment_items.question_content:
The images below depict a domestic cat and a saber-toothed cat. Domestic cats are alive today. Saber-toothed cats are extinct.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Scientists estimate that the domestic cat and saber-toothed cat lineages branched off from a common ancestor roughly 22 million years ago.

**Which features can provide evidence that domestic cats and saber-toothed cats evolved from a common ancestor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
"[Star Spangled Cat](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Star_Spangled_Cat.jpg)" by Flamingice007sg, [public domain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_domain)

"[Smilodon fatalis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Smilodon_fatalis.jpg)" by Dantheman9758 , [CC BY 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/deed.en)

options.alt: A domestic cat with four legs, a fur coat, a small, closed mouth, and a long tail.
options.caption: *a domestic cat*

options.alt: A drawing of a saber-toothed cat. The saber-toothed cat is shown with four legs, a fur coat, a large open mouth with long upper canine teeth, and a short tail.
options.caption: *a drawing of a saber-toothed cat, recreated from fossil evidence*
